sprint: Rule 5 Follow-Up -- Transparent Padding + Baked-In Rounded Corners
task: A direct, user-clarified follow-up to the Rule 5 audit, prompted by two attached screenshots (a Landscape export showing letterboxed instead of genuinely landscape-shaped, and a downloaded PNG with plain square file corners). Resolved per the user's own explicit direction: the destination's own fixed file shape (Instagram Portrait/Square) stays exactly as it was, satisfying the real platform requirement, but the padding around a non-matching Scene is now genuine alpha transparency instead of a filled letterbox bar, so the visible content reads as the Scene's own true proportions with nothing fabricated around it. _fitCompositeInto gained an opts.transparent flag, used only by the Story Carousel PNG path (which genuinely supports alpha) -- the Story Book PDF/JPEG path is untouched and still fills an opaque background, since a transparent canvas pixel composites to solid black in a JPEG encode, not white. A new _applyRoundedCorners bakes the editor's own cosmetic border-radius into the actual exported PNG pixels via destination-in compositing, proportionally scaled so the corners read at the same visual proportion the editor shows -- applied only to the Carousel PNG's final composited bitmap, not the PDF path, since a printed page's corners have no real transparency concept. Verified via two new Playwright tests confirming genuine alpha=0 transparency in the letterbox bands and all four file corners of a real Landscape Scene's Carousel export, while the Story Book/PDF path stays fully opaque and unaffected. Full regression across the existing suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to js/storyDestinations.js.
